title: NCEA Level 3 Mechanics — Practice Exam 2
standard: AS91521 — Demonstrate understanding of mechanical systems
instructions: Answer ALL questions. g = 9.81 m/s². Express numerical answers to 3 significant figures.

questions.questionText: An object moves in a circle of radius 2 m with ω = 4 rad/s. What is its centripetal acceleration?
A 5 kg mass on a spring (k = 500 N/m) oscillates with SHM. What is the angular frequency ω?
A 2000 kg car skids to a stop from 30 m/s in 4 s. What is the average braking force?
In SHM with amplitude A = 0.2 m and ω = 3 rad/s, what is the maximum acceleration?
questions.explanation: a_max = ω²A = 3² × 0.2 = 1.8 m/s² (occurs at maximum displacement).

questions.questionText: A ball is launched at 30 m/s at 60° above horizontal on level ground. (g = 9.81 m/s², sin 60° = 0.866, cos 60° = 0.5) Calculate: (a) the horizontal component of velocity, (b) the vertical component of initial velocity, (c) the time of flight, (d) the range.
questions.explanation: Horizontal and vertical components are independent. Time of flight comes from vertical, then multiplied by horizontal speed for range.

questions.questionText: A uniform rod of mass 4 kg and length 3 m rotates about its centre. (a) What is its moment of inertia? (b) A torque of 6 N·m is applied. What is the angular acceleration? (c) Starting from rest, what is its angular velocity after 5 s?
questions.explanation: Rod about centre: I = ML²/12. Then Newton's second law for rotation: τ = Iα. Finally rotational SUVAT.

questions.questionText: A 0.2 kg mass oscillates on a spring with k = 80 N/m and amplitude A = 0.1 m. (a) Find ω and period T. (b) Find the total mechanical energy. (c) Find the speed at x = 0.06 m. (d) At what displacement is the speed equal to half the maximum speed? (e) Describe the energy interchange that takes place as the mass passes through the equilibrium position.
questions.modelAnswer: (a) ω = √(k/m) = √(80/0.2) = 20 rad/s. T = 2π/ω = 2π/20 ≈ 0.314 s. (b) E = ½kA² = ½ × 80 × 0.01 = 0.4 J. (c) v = ω√(A²−x²) = 20√(0.01−0.0036) = 20√0.0064 = 20×0.08 = 1.6 m/s. (d) v = ½v_max = ½Aω = 1.0 m/s. Solve v = ω√(A²−x²): 1.0 = 20√(A²−x²) → √(A²−x²) = 0.05 → x² = 0.01 − 0.0025 = 0.0075 → x = ±0.0866 m ≈ ±86.6 mm. (e) At equilibrium all energy is kinetic (maximum KE = 0.4 J, PE = 0). As the mass passes through equilibrium, the spring is at its natural length (no extension), so PE = 0 and all stored energy is in motion.
questions.explanation: Total energy is conserved at E = ½kA². At any x, KE = E − ½kx².
